pattern: Greedily start each group at the smallest remaining card and consume the next consecutive values, which forces a valid partition into equal consecutive runs if one exists.
hints: If the total number of cards is not divisible by the group size, the answer is immediately no.
The smallest available card must begin some group, and that group is forced to take the next consecutive values.
Count card frequencies, then repeatedly take the lowest remaining value as a group start and decrement the counts of the next groupSize consecutive values; fail if any of them is missing.

edge_inputs.description: splits into three consecutive runs of three
count not divisible by group size
a gap in values blocks a straight
duplicates that cannot all pair into runs
large gap between two valid pairs

stress.description: construct several consecutive runs then shuffle, guaranteeing a valid partition
stress.note: always solvable so the greedy must succeed; values can collide across runs

stress.description: random hands that are usually unsolvable, divisibility permitting
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    g = rng.randint(2, 5)
    n = (max(1, size) // g) * g
    if n == 0:
        n = g
    hand = [rng.randint(0, 30) for _ in range(n)]
    return [hand, g]
stress.note: random values exercise the failure path
